key_points: Industries are economic activities involved in extracting natural resources, manufacturing goods, and providing services
Industrial systems operate through a structured process involving inputs, processes, and outputs
The location of industries depends on several factors including raw materials, power, labor, capital, transport, and market availability
Industrial regions develop where these factors are concentrated and infrastructure is well developed
Industrial development contributes to economic growth but may also cause environmental pollution if not managed sustainably
examples: Agro-based industry: Sugar industry using sugarcane as raw material
Mineral-based industry: Steel industry using iron ore and coal
Forest-based industry: Paper industry using timber and wood pulp
Marine-based industry: Fish processing industry using ocean resources
Industrial region example: The Mumbai–Pune industrial region in India
important_terms: Industrial System: A production system consisting of inputs, manufacturing processes, and outputs
Industrial Location: The geographical placement of industries based on economic and physical factors
Infrastructure: Basic facilities such as roads, power, communication, and transport required for industrial development
Industrial Pollution: Environmental contamination caused by industrial waste, smoke, and chemicals
Sustainable Industrial Development: Industrial growth that protects natural resources and the environment
exam_tips: Explain clearly the components of the industrial system: inputs, processes, and outputs
Understand how factors like raw materials, transport, and markets influence industrial location
Remember different types of industries based on raw materials such as agro-based, mineral-based, forest-based, and marine-based
Study the environmental impacts of industrial development including air and water pollution
Be able to explain the concept of sustainable industrial development with examples
